background: We propose to calculate a $95 \%$ confidence interval for the risk difference in the estrogen trial presented earlier. We've established that $a_1=751$ and $n_1=8506$ in the treatment group. In the control group, $a_2=623$ and $n_2=8102$.

task.query: Calculate the adjusted proportions for the treatment group and control group, including $\tilde{p}_1$ and $\tilde{p}_2$.
Calculate the standard error for the difference in adjusted proportions, $SE_{\tilde{p}_1-\tilde{p}_2}$.
Calculate the $95 \%$ confidence interval for the risk difference, $p_1-p_2$.
task.answer: The $95 \%$ confidence interval for $p_1-p_2=\tilde{p}_1-\tilde{p}_2 \pm z \cdot SE_{\hat{p}-\tilde{p}_2}= (0.08839-0.07700) \pm(1.96)(0.004271)=0.01139 \pm 0.00837 =(0.00302$ to 0.01976$)$, or approximately from $0.3 \%$ to $2.0 \%$.
